title: Gimmick
text:
The ad is far more interesting than the actual item. Very inaccurate readings. So far off that it appears that it just makes stuff up. Which I tend to believe. For one, you cannot obtain a blood pressure reading in this manner. It doesn't just give heart rate and blood oxygen readings unless you go to that screen and activate it. And it takes a long time to read anything, or rather make up some random number.
Final words; please don't waste your money. This is a toy for children.

title: Piénselo, cómprelo, úselo y tome su decisión
text: Estéticamente no está mal aunque prefería un  reloj de forma redonda... No estoy seguro si las lecturas de latidos, Presión sanguinea y pasos en realidad son verdaderas o es algo aleatorio del reloj. Sin embargo parecieran estar bastante próximas a la realidad. La aplicación nativa no es lo suficientemente buena, de hecho pudiera ser mejor, no se puede aumentar o disminuir el tamaño de las letras y las de fábrica son muy pequeñas. El manual es casi ilegible. Las opciones para el cambio de pantalla no son las mejores y no se pueden agregar otras distintas a las que trae ni se pueden eliminar las pre configuradas. La duración de la batería no está mal. El sistema de carga a través de una unión magnética está interesante. La lectura de los diferentes mensajes es perfecta a menos que recibas muchos alertas, lo que hará que se vayan borrando los anteriores. No estoy seguro si la medición de la presión sanguínea es real, creo que es algo preconfigurado. El conteo de pasos difiere de la aplic

title: Well worth the $30.
text: I would like to start with. 3 months owning the watch.  I am in no way affiliated with this product or company.  I am a former FF/EMT- that will come towards the BP, HR, 02 and sleep tracker that many in the reviews discredit. First off, people don't understand that you have to read instructions.  I know my normal vitals from day to day activities. If you raise the watch to heart level.  It is very accurate- from my watch.  From my sleep studies- this watch is very accurate.  I originally purchased this watch to be able to control music without taking out my phone and tell time. That was worth $30 to me.  I have found the blood pressure,  oxygen,  heart rate,  sleep tracker to be accurate.  My complaint is sometimes I need to open (and close) the app on my phone in order for it to connect data to control music- inconvenient for what I bought it for.  But with the rest of the conveniences on the watch, I'm still very happy with the purchase.  I haven't tested the exercise tracker- no ne

title: GREAT Watch but no sleep tracker
text:
So far this watch has been an amazing piece of inexpensive tech. I needed a heart rate monitor for workouts, and a cleanly designed watch for work, so this fit the bill perfectly.
I love the function of being able to change the wallpaper on it! It seems like such a small thing, but sometimes being able to add just that little bit of customization makes a HUGE difference in morale.

The double secured straps on the band make this a great nod to those of us with tiny wrists. No floppy strap!

The only downside is mine did not come with the Sleep tracker function. I attempted to contact the company and have not gotten any replies yet... It is concerning, but the other aspects of the watch are great and work fine, so I can utilize it until I make contact.

title: Good Smart watch for not a lot of money.
text:
Had a problem with the Weather function on my watch. Contacted company and they immediately answered. They advised to download newest version of HeroBandIII on my Android phone...which I did. Now my watch is working perfectly and I’m pleased.
With their one yr warranty and their good price...it is a very good Smart watch. From what I have noticed, I believe the functions are pretty accurate. Now, if you want to spend hundreds of dollars for a Smart watch...then do it. But for me...this fits my needs pretty well.

title: I Gave Up!
text: First off, I was only interested in a fitness monitor - not necessarily a tracker. I didn't want to have to use my phone at all and it wasn't clear from responses to this question whether downloading the phone app was imperative or not.  That didn't even matter because once I charged the watch and put it on, the screen went berserk!  Different screens kept popping on and off the watch as I simply moved my wrist.  I did get a RHR and bp reading, but the readings I got were way off.  I'm a nurse and I know how to take my pulse and bp.  This has to be the most bizarre and frustrating electronic device I have ever encountered. I returned it.

title: Gift for my husband
text: He loves it. Comes with a charger in case you are wondering....it wasn't listed on the product sales!

title: Very inaccurate.
text:
Step counter is way off. Even if I stay in bed all day, it will count 1000 steps.

Heart-rate monitor is very inaccurate. Heart rate barely goes above 100bpm while playing basketball???

The best thing about this watch, is the sleep tracker. But who knows how accurate the results are.

Battery lasts about 6-days before it requires a charge.

title: Not good for Blood Pressure
text: Blood pressure measurement totally inaccurate.

title: Great Smartwatch!
text:
Fellow Amazon Shoppers:
My film director wanted me to order 5 of these, so I did. We had 1 from another brand but I was not completely happy with it. So I feel I got awfully lucky with this choice. It does it all and the price was incredible at about $30 a piece! Are you kidding me!?!?!
It was a very easy set up(I turned my Bluetooth off and as I progressed it asked me to turn it on), it syncs beautifully and I love the way it charges(with a magnet charging end to the back of the watch). Btw it came with 90% power!
The features are quite amazing:
Steps-walking-running-skipping
Blood Pressure
Heart Rate-BPM
ECG
Blood Oxygen
Sleep Monitoring
Alarm
It can take camera pics as well & make videos(it does so from your phone but you control/click from watch
It can alert you to phone calls and texts from several apps like WeChat, Instagram, Facebook etc...
This is a must buy for anyone wanting one of these type of devices. It appe

title: Basic, but very nice!
text: Basic functions, nothing fancy - which I like.  Very easy to navigate.  Step counting with all the smart watches will never be 100% accurate as they count arm movement as well (washing hands, brushing teeth, combing hair, etc.)  Sleep function seems to be fairly accurate.  Find Your Phone function is great!  Incoming messages, mail, phone calls - not very good.  Watch faces - there are plenty to choose from, but you will not be able to use your own photo for the watch face.  If you're looking for something more detailed with loads of exercise modes, using your own photo for watch face, flashlight feature - this is NOT the watch for you.  But, if you're looking for something that's very, very simple and performs the basics, this will do it!

title: Not Enough History
text: Not enough History to share with my Dr. My cuff BP was always different than watch I never knew what to use…ultimately had to send it back…😢

title: Alright
text:
Initially bought for a step tracker.
It counted steps while sleeping and while in the car.
It did not track steps If I shook my arm which is good.
Battery life is pretty decent. Also does find your phone which is cool.

title: Ehhh
text:
If you only have $30, then buy this for BP and oxygen monitoring, but…
If you have higher expectations:
Cons:
* Does not connect to wifi
* App is ehh. It actually says in the fine print it COLLECTS your user IDs from your phone. Can you tell me why an app would need to do this???? Along with contacts and their info.
* the faces on the phone are limited to 3 options
* weather did not work for me. The weather does not display on home screen
* BP option has a delay when you ask it to detect so does oxygen
* serious issues pairing with phone. For example if you’re looking for your phone and need to send a ping to find it. Mine never worked.
* on the face, when transitioning from bp to oxygen or others, it is not smooth
* does not wake up on taps to screen
* no bluetooth easy connection to phone. In fact, it would say connected but did not get the phone’s notification on thw watch

Pros:
Its cheap
It keeps up with b

title: Costo/beneficio buenísimo, recomendado
text: Lo compre para referencia médica tanto del pulso como la presión arterial, las lecturas en reposo son bastantes precisas (compare con medidores específicos), no así cuando se está en movimiento, duración de batería 3 días sin las funciones de monitoreo activadas.

title: Reloj tensiometro
text: Es un regalo para mi hermano y le gusto mucho para controlar su presión, pulso y oxigenación sanguínea

title: Thought it was perfect until....
text: When I unboxed this watch I was very excited about being able to use it. It is very stylish looking you can change the faces on it if you want with some of your own pictures which is a nice added bonus, but it does not keep correct steps. I am a driver.,  And when I turn the steering wheel so many times it will reflect on my step count. I have to use another app along with the watch now launch now to check and see what the difference is this and I was hoping I wouldn't have to carry my phone with me at all times that's why I purchased this. It is a very nice watch and if you're looking for something that or something that looks nice that's fine or if you're looking for something that gives you more steps than what you really do then it's great but I would go to another other watch if you're looking for something that tracks correctly.

title: NOT WORTH IT
text: Less than 2 months in and it stopped working. Will not charge or turn on.

title: Bad battery
text:
Received the smartwatch as promised. Smartwatch was very easy to set up. I like the fact that it provides me with my BP, HR, BO and monitors my sleep. The only issue so far that I have is that it did not come with a screen protector and I am having a hard time trying to find the right one for the watch, but other than that it is a great device.
********Edited review January 31st **********
Smartwatch is suppose to have an excellent battery. I'm not sure why, but the battery barely last a day before I have to charge it again.

title: Piece of junk
text: It didn't work.  Bought it mainly for the blood pressure monitor and according to it my blood pressure was always the same no matter what I ate or what time of day I used it.  Differing greatly from the bulky blood pressure cuff I was trying to avoid traveling with; that gave accurate readings. Not worth the money or the wait to get it at all.

title: Maybe 6 total charge cycles at most and watch is dead
text:
Watch was barely used. Spent most of it's time in a drawer. Just had surgery and wanted to get steppin. Only wanted it for heart rate and step counts. I know BP and O2 on these things are next to useless. This watch is useless because it is dead and won't take a charge. Just nothing. Watch is dead. Too late to return.
NOT IMPRESSED.

title: Junk...quit working after 2 months
text: I wouldn't recommend this watch to anyone.  The sleep tracker quit working after about a month. The weather is never accurate and keeps flipping to Celsius. When sleep mode did work, it would.show me sleeping when I was awake. I know it's a cheap watch, which makes it appealing if you can't afford more expensive one, but you might as well burn your $30.

title: Smart watch
text: Works great,this watch will track your day time sleep too.Thanks love it.

title: Great watch
text: This watch dies all the same functions as a watch costing 10 times.

title: For a cheap tracker it is great
text: Compared at Dr.s office and pulse BP and oxygen were fairly close

title: GREAT VALUE
text: It is exactly as expected, but I did not read the description properly.  You can receive your text messages on this phone, but you cannot text back.  You cannot get calls on this phone either.  It's more of a fitbit.  But, it's a great for what it is.

title: Great Affordable Smart Watch
text: This is a great little affordable smartwatch that is also easy to use. I downloaded the app for the watch and was on my way. Very impressed by the app and the ability to keep track of exercise, blood pressure, heart rate, and oxygen levels. Also impressed with the watch's ability to track your steps during the day and let you know if you are reaching your exercise goal. The battery life is good. Better than another smartwatch purchased 3 years ago. Went more than a week before needed a charge. My previous watch required charging daily.

title: Wonderful watch for the price
text: I loved this watch for a few weeks.  But then got a burn on my arm after charging it.  Not good for people allergic to metal.  My husband has the same watch and has had no problem.

title: Excellent value
text: I am pleased with this purchase.  BP, HR, O2, Steps are all accurate within their intended use.  I have compared this watch's results with other specifically designed devices and all are within acceptable tolerances (for me anyway).  Sleep tracking is OK, if you turn on continuous heart rate monitoring.  Product says "The watch's default sleep monitoring time is from 9 p.m. to 9 a.m. and cannot be changed."  But mine starts at 10:30pm every time.  Not a show stopper, but definiiely an annoyance. Battery life is outstanding. I am 2 1/2 weeks into the first charge and still at 50%.  The APP is simple to set up and a pleasure to use.  Apart from the problem with the sleep tracking , I would highly recommend this watch.

title: Not accurate
text: This thing is all over the place! Sometimes it comes sort of close to what my cuff reads, other times it's WAY off. Heartrate is the same way. Add to this a weird rash I'm getting from the back of it and it's not worth it. I'm sending it back!

title: inacturacy of the blood pressure results
text: checking my blood pressure during the day. I have had a heart attack and need to check my pressure, I find this equipment reads low

title: Works great
text: I get my BP and steps right on the watch!

title: Better Than Previous One Used
text:
Works fine. Connects to android easily and the charging is fast.
Only concern is how easy it will be getting a replacement charging cable in Nigeria.

title: Good for a beginner
text: I don't know how accurate it is. But I will say it's nice to have a basic step counter Bp and heart rate.  It's not waterproof from what I gathered. But for a first time watch counter it does a good job.

title: HAPPY Girl! 🤗
text: Pop glory is a beautiful Watch for the Money. It takes about a day. For the phone to completely upgrade. But overall it was simple and I didn't need help from the Teens. Not a fan of the Silicone Wristband..because I can see where its starting to get  dirt on it. So just order a bunch of Silicone bands or a few leather bands.

title: Love it, watch band is NOT durable.
text: I absolutely love this watch however within a month watch strap broke for the first time. I was able to fix this however 2 more months later it's irreparably broken. Super sad cause it does everything I need it to and worked nice for me.

title: Gift
text: A gift for my mama. It doesn’t work right. Probably returning

title: It's a good product
text: My family member who I gave this watch to is very happy with the watch it tracks your blood pressure their beats per minute and their sleep patterns it also measures their daily pedometer steps

title: Amazing!
text: Great and fashionable product. I use it everyday

title: Not a smart watch at all!
text: It was working fine until I went to the West Coast. When I returned it was still showing West Coast time no matter how many times I deleted and downloaded the app on the phone. I also turned the watch on and off multiple times. It just doesn't want to sync. If anyone has had a similar experience and was successful, pl let me know what you did. I want to contact the seller but there is no info regarding that or it has disappeared. Thanks in advance for any help getting my watch back to its original programing.

title: Cute watch
text: This is such a cute watch. I'd buy it again.
